question: [File: 'File Name: rest/config.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: loadYaml()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a YAML file containing a billion-laughs anchor expansion (e.g., deeply nested aliases) to `yaml.load()` causing unbounded memory allocation that exhausts the REST service heap, preventing the node from processing any new transaction confirmations?
